artifact.name: BD_07_ADVISOR_APPROVAL_NOT_RELEASE_REPORT.json
artifact.title: Advisor approval is not compliance release

ticket_contract.work_type: Business Rule / Release Semantics / Guard Proof
ticket_contract.unique_shape: Semantic hardening ticket; small UI possible, but the real work is rule separation and negative proof.
ticket_contract.execution_decision: Implement only after rule labels and forbidden transitions are specified.

completed_execution_sequence.validation: No inspected state name implies client release before compliance release.
Advisor-approved but unreleased cases remain hidden from client projection.

process_first_result.decision: BD-07 is satisfied by semantic state/proof lock over existing implementation; no product code change was needed in this slice.
process_first_result.reason: The repository already has explicit WorkflowGate, ReleaseSpine, typed command bus and UI-copy evidence that advisor approval is necessary but insufficient.
process_first_result.screenshot_rationale: No UI changed; screenshot would only restate existing copy and would not prove forbidden transitions.

source_evidence.role: Client visibility and compliance release guards.
Advisor approval and compliance release command semantics.
UI copy and control hierarchy for advisor/compliance release boundaries.
Semantic client-visibility and release negative tests.
Command-path proof for explicit evidence, advisor approval and compliance release gates.
UI proof for evidence lifecycle, advisory boundary and compliance gates.

commands_run.command: pnpm playwright test tests/workflow-gate.spec.ts tests/release-spine-command-surface.spec.ts tests/client-visibility-projection.spec.ts tests/true-ux-p0-safety.spec.ts tests/scf-p04-p06-flow-ui.spec.ts tests/journey-api.spec.ts --workers=1

validation.positive_acceptance_result: Compliance release succeeds only through explicit release preconditions and command path.
validation.negative_acceptance_result: Advisor approval alone cannot expose client payloads, mark release complete, create export readiness or create client acceptance.

facts: canBecomeClientVisible requires recommendationStatus RELEASED_TO_CLIENT and complianceStatus RELEASED in addition to advisor approval.
ReleaseSpine requires advisor approval plus evidence, audit, redaction, rationale, payload, permission and data-quality readiness.
typed-workflow-command-bus sets advisor approval to COMPLIANCE_PENDING with clientVisible false.
compliance_release is the path that can set RELEASED_TO_CLIENT and clientVisible true after precondition checks.

killed_weak_branches.branch: Treat advisor approval as enough for client visibility.
killed_weak_branches.reason: Violates no-unapproved-advice and is explicitly blocked by workflow-gate tests.

killed_weak_branches.branch: Use UI copy only to explain the distinction.
killed_weak_branches.reason: Copy without command/state proof would be a cosmetic shield over a dangerous semantic shortcut.

killed_weak_branches.branch: Let admin or compliance checklist status imply release.
killed_weak_branches.reason: DG-02 and ReleaseSpine require explicit release command, permission and audit persistence.

adversarial_qa.attack: Advisor approves and client sees advice.
adversarial_qa.defense: client visibility gate still requires compliance_release and RELEASED_TO_CLIENT.

adversarial_qa.attack: Checklist says approved and release becomes implied.
adversarial_qa.defense: release action and audit persistence remain required.

adversarial_qa.attack: Export becomes available because advisor approved.
adversarial_qa.defense: ReleaseSpine export readiness requires release plus export approval, redaction, target and clean payload.

bold_cleanup_recommendations: Create a central release-state-label formatter and remove ad hoc approval/release wording from page components.
Ban unqualified 'Approved' on all release-adjacent surfaces unless the adjacent field names exactly what was approved.
Make direct clientVisible writes outside compliance_release a test failure.
Promote the forbidden transition matrix into a reusable fixture for every process-first workflow.
remaining_limits: No UI text was changed in this slice because existing surfaces already carry the safety boundary and tests pass.
A later UI-copy hardening slice should still replace generic compliance 'Approved' badges with more specific labels.
BD-07 does not remove historical alternate release proof docs; it locks the active process-first rule.
